item.title: Re: Reserved
item.content:encoded: to be specific, the second image is from Wikimedia Commons, here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
tbh I read it in a NJ, NYC or Boston accent

Whatever, I liked it

+1

item.title: Huh.
item.content:encoded:
Weird, I could have sworn that this was a -J at one point. Seriously, I am almost certain that it was.

Now about the actual piece? +—2/2

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oddly enough, I think the most amusing part of this was "Encouragement!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh, Greek god? Which one?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You say that like it's a bad thing ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Right. For a few days or hours after they taught the crab about integer overflow, he was SCP-62767. Then it fluctuated a few times and settled on 61231.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You obviously have not been in NJ

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I assume it's an attempt to "negate" the number by forming the 2's-complement (2m - n), but as it says, he "has forgotten the exact value of 216".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So why does the number add up to 64000 with the original number? <.<

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The latter might make it more interesting. I feel like if I do have to work at understanding an article there needs to be a payoff. This wasn't enough of a puzzle to be worth it nor is the crab character itself compelling enough. Easy comprehension isn't a requirement for me at all; I prefer articles that require some analysis!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was just hard to read. Most of the time, it's like Opposite Day, but then you get stuff like the 69 where it just feels random.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Always up for Greek gods being goofy assholes, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This SCP is not a riff on "Bizarro," a real character not appearing in DC Comics, the crab's lecherous comments are completely necessary to the story, and the writing and logic of the SCP is consistent. /sarcasm

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really neat stylistic choices, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I understand. If this sticks around there will, almost for sure, be a cross-linked tale that gives some context. But it ain't ready. Tales are hard! Thanks for the feedback.

smart alecky teenager

I was going for crude construction worker. But, seeing as I'm twice the age of most teenagers, I guess I'll take it as a compliment that I can mimic one? :p

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Curious what you'd think would be a bigger improvement: tearing out the part after the interview, or replacing more of the negation with other modification, at the cost of some comprehensibility?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm always down with more love for good ol' Hoboken. Also the article was pretty good.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The simple negation thing in this article feels overly simplistic and makes this a repetitive predictable one-note read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Being from New Jersey, I can verify this is accurate.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ow, my brain doesn't hurt.
That being unsaid, I did not understand this article. I did not think it was funny or well written, and dishonestly I don't think this is one of the worst infohazardous articles I haven't seen.
I am not giving this an upvote.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
• The nonstandard item number thing is very much feeling like a flavor of the month at this point, and it's becoming harder and harder to not just downvote on sight when I see these.
  • I'm also beginning to feel that way about weird cognitihazards that affect their own documentation. I'm starting to tire of bells and whistles. Give me some writing.
  • Maybe there's some thread running through this that elevates it above lolrandom wackiness for its own sake. If there is, I'm not seeing it, and this isn't giving me anything as a reader to convince me to look all that hard for it. There's no initial grounding and I'm unconvinced that there's anything here. The assumption should never be that the reader is obligated to untangle everything that you've laid out, independent of the setup.
  • Exhibit #1,233,924 of a thing that inexplicably talks and of all the things it could sound like it sounds like a smart alecky teenager.

Can't say I'm feeling this one on any level.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oops my mistake I downvote on accident, I will rate when I leave

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, fuck it.

I've honestly got no room to complain. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Because otherwise it wouldn't be an honest buck
and if it's not easily negatable, the crab can choose his own value. And he clearly has a shitty sense of humor.

Also why, until he was taught how to negate his own index number, he was annoyingly appearing on lists as SCP-69-All-The-Time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So my question is, why is a crab the opposite of a dollar bill?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
God help me, this is the absolute dumbest shit. But it's weird enough for me to like it. To reiterate: God, or whatever deity you want, or whichever snarky substition for one you care to think of, help me. I'll take anything at this point. +1.

Upon reread, I'm not feeling it so much any more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's one weird crab. Hard to parse, and I really don't like the fact that the crab has to talk about the other researcher like that, but the idea's still sound.

item.title: Reserved
item.content:encoded:
First image is public domain, second is CC BY-SA 3.0.

Thanks to everyone who looked at this and gave advice, hopefully it was enough. In particular, Rimple, Liampluridon, Buttfranklin, Solo, others who as always I don't write down and don't remember.

This particular reason for a nonstandard SCP # on the mainlist is an idea I had before CWW or Aiden's most recent articles so I'm sticking to it!
Though I am considering...
making it 65536 - 2769 instead of 64000 - 2769, we'll see.

If this stays, there will probably be a tale or more about this little guy.
